semantic.role: Low-level configuration resolver for pre-commit hook enablement within the Infra Config layer. It centralizes the decision logic between environment variable overrides and explicit configuration, providing a structured resolution result to consumers.
responsibilities: Check the SPINE_PRE_COMMIT environment variable first to determine pre-commit enablement, giving it highest priority.
Fall back to the explicit boolean parameter when the environment variable is not set, enabling or disabling pre-commit accordingly.
Return a BooleanSettingResolution object that clearly indicates the resolved value and its source (env var or explicit parameter).
Abstract away the environment variable name and parsing logic, keeping the implementation details hidden from callers.
outOfScope: Loading or writing configuration files to disk.
Handling any configuration settings other than pre-commit enablement.
Any UI, CLI interaction, or user-facing prompts.
Direct interaction with the database or persistence layer.
invariants: Environment variable (SPINE_PRE_COMMIT) override always takes precedence over the explicit boolean argument.
The module uses the PRE_COMMIT_ENV_VAR constant from env.js to avoid hardcoding the variable name.
No side effects: the function only reads process.env and returns a value; it does not mutate state or perform I/O beyond environment variable access.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Part of the Infra Config layer that isolates environment-specific pre-commit configuration logic into a dedicated module, keeping the decision policy clear and testable.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Refined or added the resolvePreCommitSetting function to support structured resolution output with source tracking, aligning with enhanced configuration diagnostics in ArchSpine v1.0.0.

publicSurface.description: Determines whether pre-commit hooks should be enabled by checking the SPINE_PRE_COMMIT environment variable first, falling back to the provided boolean parameter. Returns a BooleanSettingResolution object with value and source fields.
